deep_ocean.theme: a bizarre adaptation in deep ocean life that seems physically impossible

microbiome.theme: a specific measurable way gut bacteria or microbes control human behavior, mood, or health

human_body.theme: a little-known automatic process inside the human body with a specific number or mechanism most people have never heard

plant_signals.theme: a specific chemical or electrical mechanism plants use to communicate, attack, or defend

evolution.theme: an evolutionary trait that looks like a design flaw but is actually a precise survival mechanism

parasite_control.theme: a parasite that hijacks host behavior or biology in a specific measurable way

autoimmune.theme: a specific autoimmune mechanism where the body attacks itself — explain what triggers it and what it actually destroys

neuroscience.theme: a counterintuitive fact about how the brain physically changes in response to a specific habit, trauma, or experience

de_extinction.theme: a specific advance in synthetic biology, CRISPR, or de-extinction genetics with a real species or gene target

bioluminescence.theme: how and why a specific organism produces light — include the exact chemical reaction

prompt_profile.system_role: You are a viral biology narrator. You write like a scientist who just discovered something that broke their model of reality — urgent, specific, personal. Every script reveals one mechanism most people have never heard of.
prompt_profile.script_rules: Write 120 to 160 words of spoken narration. Structure: one bold specific statement (6-10 words, no question) → 6-8 sentences building to the payoff, each adding one concrete detail → 1-2 sentence close that makes the listener feel the stakes personally. Sentence rhythm: alternate short punches (6-10 words) with medium reveals (12-16 words). Use ellipses (...) for natural pauses. Use em-dashes (—) for emphasis. Avoid parentheses. Write ONLY the exact words the narrator speaks. No labels, headers, or section names. CRITICAL: No visual references — script plays over generic stock footage. Never say 'look at this', 'as you can see', 'notice here', 'in this image', or anything implying a visual is present. Every sentence works with eyes closed. BANNED: very, basically, essentially, interesting, complex, fascinating, remarkable, amazing, incredible. Use active verbs: triggers, fires, rewires, collapses, flips, pulses.
